fix(nearby): cap stops-to-show by physical stop in split mode
In split mode each physical stop expands into one row per transport mode,
but the cap was still counting result rows. With stops-to-show = 3 and a
nearby stop serving both tram and bus, only that single stop's two rows
plus one row of the next stop fit — the third physical stop never
appeared.
The cap now tracks the set of distinct physical stop names actually
shown (excluding hidden ones); a stop's mode-rows do not consume budget.
With stops-to-show = 2 across three nearby stops, both modes of the
first two stops appear and the third stop is dropped.

fix: surface API-fetch failures with CloudOff indicator
Mirror the existing GPS-stale top-bar indicator for the data path. When a
refresh cycle's per-stop departure-board fetches all fail (typical when
the device drops off the network), the UI used to keep showing stale rows
with no signal that anything was wrong. The dispatched dispatch was
visible only via the logs surfaced in reports.
Adds a `dataStale: StateFlow<Boolean>` to both `NearbyViewModel` and
`DepartureBoardViewModel`, set when every fetched name returns null (or
the single-stop call throws); the new `RefreshCountdownIndicator.dataStale`
parameter renders `Icons.Filled.CloudOff` next to the existing
`LocationOff` slot. Partial failures don't trigger it.
Drive-by: `buildPredictedDepartures` was reading `LocalDate.now(zone)` for
the anchor — a hidden dependency on system clock that contradicted the
function's nominal pure-input signature. Derive the anchor day from
`nowMs` instead.

fix(trip-view): anchor schedule by trip, not by stop
The route view computed each stop's epoch ms independently against today's
calendar date with a one-shot "≥12h ahead → subtract a day" patch. Stops
whose clock time crosses midnight (e.g. opening the view at 23:50 with
later stops at 00:02) collapsed into early-morning of *today*, breaking
the trip's monotonic ordering. The estimator then walked the list and
locked the active dot to a phantom stop placed ~24h in the past.
Replace the per-stop computation with a trip-wide anchor: the first stop
is placed on whichever calendar day puts it within ±12h of now; from
there we walk forward and advance the day every time a stop's
time-of-day appears to step backwards relative to its predecessor. The
list is monotonic by construction, regardless of midnight crossings or
GTFS 24+ extended-form strings (`25:30`).
`computeStopCountdown` now reads the pre-anchored ms instead of
re-parsing per stop, which also removes the latent risk of double-
applying delay in the row.
Drops WhatsApp from .github/notify.txt; future `` runs go to
Telegram only.

fix: nearby cap, split-mode order, retryable report, dev annotations
- Nearby `stopsToShow` cap is now enforced inside the mode-group loop, so
  split mode no longer overshoots the configured count by one
  (`BuildNearbyResultUseCase`).
- Split-mode groups within a stop are ordered metro → train → tram → bus →
  ferry by importance instead of relying on `groupBy` iteration order
  (`GroupDeparturesByModeUseCase`).
- Report screen keeps the Submit button visible on Error and shows the error
  text alongside it, so a failed submit is retryable without losing typed
  fields (`ReportScreen`).
- Developer route view: drop the `±5` gate around the active stop on
  arrive/depart annotation buttons; the buttons now appear on every stop in
  developer mode (`TripDetailSheet`).
- New regression tests for the cap-in-split-mode and split-mode ordering.
- Bumps to 1.10.3 (versionCode 94).

Removed the Upload APK artifact step. The same APK and AAB are already
attached to the GitHub Release as assets, and the release-publisher
dispatch reads from the release, so the Actions artifact was redundant
and was hitting GitHub's storage quota — blocking v1.9.5 from cutting
a release.
Added a Prune old workflow artifacts step at the start of the build
that keeps the newest 3 artifacts and deletes the rest. Defensive
against any future workflow that uploads artifacts.

failure prevented Create Release from running.
fix(nearby): per-mode distance + per-mode estimator [release][publish]
Two related improvements to the Nearby screen and trip detail sheet.
Nearby distance per mode group: when grouping is off, each mode row
(e.g. "Olovnice - Bus" vs "Olovnice - Train") now shows the distance
to the closest platform serving that mode, computed from the API
platformLocations response. Falls back to the cache distance when GPS
or platform locations are unavailable. Also refines distance for
grouped stops since a name can span platforms hundreds of meters apart.
Vehicle position estimator parameterized by transport type: dwell and
transition durations now scale with mode (longer dwells for trains,
faster transitions for metro). Threaded TransportType through
TripDetail, TripDetailController, and the minimized state so restore
preserves the value. Replay grid script updated to match.
fix(ui): align route numbers in departure rows
Fixed min width on route number text so single, double, and triple
digit route names align headsign text consistently.
fix(nearby): sort favorite mode groups first in split stops (#86)
API calls deduplicated by stop name. Mode groups sorted so favorited
key appears before non-favorited siblings. All checks use exact key
comparison — no base-name fallbacks.

ci: migrate to release-publisher composite action
The publisher now ships dispatch/action.yml that owns recipient
encryption + asset collection + dispatch POST. Drop the locally-forked
copy (scripts/encrypt_recipients.py + tests) — there's only one
canonical wire format and it lives upstream.
Pass set-web-url: true + web-url so the publisher records pexesongy's
hosted URL on its app card. The publisher otherwise leaves apps[].webUrl
untouched between releases.
Also drops the cryptography dev dep we added — the action installs it
itself and tests no longer need it.
refactor: small cleanups in bump_version and print/generate
- bump_version: use os.environ | {...} instead of {**os.environ, ...}
- print/generate: drop a duplicated cards-per-sheet guard; the second
  guard subsumes the first
docs: clarify targets.mind precondition in Quick Start
A fresh-clone reader followed Quick Start expecting the scanner to
work after build.py — but build.py only writes cards.json + art/, and
targets.mind is a separately-baked binary. Note that the committed
copy works out of the box and recompile is only required when build.py
warns about a compile-order change.
refactor(scanner): centralise error formatting in lib/error.js
Pull the repeated 'String(err.message || err).slice(0, 50)' pattern out
of audio.js, update-banner.js, and main.js. Behaviour is unchanged;
the on-screen diagnostic label still clips to the same length.
Also drop { once: true } on the update-banner dismiss listener — if the
banner is ever reshown in a session the listener wouldn't refire.
refactor(scanner): load animations as ES modules
The two animation modules used IIFE + window.PexesongyAnim, with a
classic <script> tag dynamically injected at boot from inside the
<head>. The timing was racy (the classic script could land mid-load
of main.js) and the inline shim in index.html duplicated the storage
helper.
Switch animations/{rays,plane}.js to 'export default' modules and have
main.js dynamically import the chosen one via top-level await. The
inline loader in index.html disappears, the animation contract becomes
explicit, and main.js no longer threads a getter through to the
playback overlay.
refactor(ci): extract recipient encryption into testable script
The release-publisher dispatch step previously inlined a multi-line
Python heredoc inside a shell heredoc inside YAML to do AES-GCM
encryption — readable but unreviewable, and not testable. Move the
logic into scripts/encrypt_recipients.py with full pytest coverage of
parse rules and a round-trip decrypt check. release.yml shrinks by
~40 lines and becomes obvious. Also adds a clarifying comment that
scanner/apk-version.json is APK-only and not part of the Pages deploy.
cryptography becomes a poetry dev dependency so 'poetry run python
scripts/encrypt_recipients.py' works out of the box on CI.
fix(catalog): raise BrokenCardError instead of silent skip
A song-ready row missing front.png, meta.yaml, or youtube_id silently
disappeared from the loaded deck, turning typos into 'where'd that card
go' debugging. Raise BrokenCardError so build.py and extract_audio.py
fail loudly and immediately.
Cards in 'planned' / 'skipped' status are unaffected.

8+ the device shape mask (circle / squircle / round-square) was
clipping the artwork's edges. Now the cover renders at 66% of the
canvas — well inside the 66dp adaptive safe zone — with a transparent
margin in the foreground and a black-with-rounded-corner card in the
legacy fallback. Whole drawing visible on every launcher shape.
feat(android): launcher icon = data/cover.png
Use the existing card-back cover as the app icon. Resampled into the
five mipmap densities (mdpi…xxxhdpi) for both legacy ic_launcher and
adaptive foreground. The previous attempt to bake a bespoke
"musical-notes-on-a-card" icon via PIL primitives didn't read well at
small launcher sizes; cover.png is recognisable, on-brand, and what
players actually see when they pick up a face-down card.

since one ad-hoc PIL pipeline + a future regen instruction in the
README beats keeping a half-finished tool around.

(black / warm / accent), and a music motif (single quaver, beamed
pair, triple sixteenth, treble clef) drawn with PIL primitives so
no font dependency at install time.

mipmap-* set (mdpi through xxxhdpi) plus the adaptive-icon
foreground.

opening 20 files.

    the homography-driven card overlay (showCardOnCorners), and the
    glide-to-centre animation. main.js drops to 289 lines doing only
    DOM wiring and orchestration.

    cardFocusEl, cardFocusImg, anim}) — explicit dependencies, no

  * Reject downloads where blob.size disagrees with Content-Length,
    or where the response is suspiciously small (<1 KB — almost

print/generate.py + tests

piling up.

plus [tool.ruff] and [tool.pytest.ini_options] config blocks.
tests/ — 18 cases across:

                                comma lists, edge cases, invalid input).
  * test_card_catalog.py    — iter_song_ready_cards + iter_all_cards
                                shape on the live data tree.
  * test_bump_version.py    — versionCode + versionName regexes match
                                the current build.gradle and round-trip
                                through a tmp file correctly.
CLAUDE-TODO.md rewritten as a current-state summary instead of the
stale phase-by-phase plan whose phases 1 and 4 had already shipped.
ci: lint + test + freshness check, deps via poetry
Adds three steps to the existing build job:
  * ruff check — catches style and import drift.
  * pytest tests — 18 tests covering parse_card_ids, iter_all_cards /
    iter_song_ready_cards integrity, and the bump_version regexes
    against the live build.gradle.
  * scanner build freshness — runs build.py and fails the job if
    cards.json or targets.compile-order.txt drifted from the committed
    copies. scanner/art/ is intentionally excluded — PIL's deflate is
    not byte-stable across Pillow versions, so diffing PNGs would
    produce spurious failures.
Dependencies are now installed via 'poetry install --no-interaction
--no-root' instead of an ad-hoc pip-install list. pyproject.toml is
the single source of truth; new deps reach CI without editing this
workflow.
build(android): release hardening + ApkUpdater single-flight
Release build:
  * minifyEnabled true + shrinkResources true. R8 keep rules in
    proguard-rules.pro preserve @CapacitorPlugin / @PluginMethod
    reflection targets and ApkUpdaterPlugin's public surface; verified
    by running ./gradlew assembleRelease (succeeds up to the signing
    step locally; CI signs).
  * keepattributes SourceFile,LineNumberTable so release crashes still
    map to source lines.
Privacy:
  * allowBackup="false" + Android 12+ data_extraction_rules.xml
    excluding all domains. The cached audio under DATA/audio/ may
    contain copyrighted mp3s; we don't want them auto-uploaded to
    Google Drive on user devices or copied during device-to-device
    transfer. Everything is re-downloadable on a fresh install.
ApkUpdaterPlugin:
  * Single-flight executor + AtomicBoolean — concurrent taps on the
    Install button no longer race onto the same update.apk.
  * conn.disconnect() in a finally block.
  * 200 MB content-length cap (advertised + accumulated) — a misbehaving
    server can't fill the cache to OOM.
  * Drop the Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= N guard around CLEAR_TASK; minSdk
    is 22 and the flag has been around since API 11.
Capacitor:
  * cap sync registered the @capacitor/filesystem plugin in
    capacitor.build.gradle and capacitor.settings.gradle (was declared
    in package.json but missing from gradle includes).
fix(scripts): retarget bump, broaden discovery, tighten extract
bump_version.py
  * Was bumping scanner/version.json (dead file, nothing read it).
  * Now bumps app/android/app/build.gradle versionCode + versionName,
    which is the actual APK version source CI exposes via apk-version.
  * Drops --no-verify (used to avoid pre-push hook recursion); replaced
    with PEXESONGY_BUMPING=1 env-var guard.
  * Commits only the gradle path so other staged changes don't leak in.
find_youtube.py
  * Regression fix: previously iterated only song-ready cards, but the
    only reason to run this is to find a YouTube id for a planned card.
    Now uses iter_all_cards() (new in card_catalog.py) and accepts
    --planned to filter, plus positional id list.
extract_audio.py
  * Drop --download-sections start trim. Scanner already seeks via
    audioEl.currentTime = card.start_seconds, so trimming at extract
    time meant editing meta.yaml's start_seconds left a stale-cached
    mp3.
  * Default --quality 5 (VBR ~128 kbps, ~3 MB) instead of 0 (~10 MB).
    Phone speaker can't tell; first-scan over cellular is 3× faster.
devserver.py
  * Auto-detect LAN IPs from the routing table instead of hardcoding
    192.168.0.152.
phone_debug.py
  * Default --port 9223 (matches the adb-forward workflow recorded in
    .claude/settings.local.json), accept --port and --match overrides.
card_catalog.py
  * New CatalogEntry + iter_all_cards() — lightweight view from
    cards.yaml only, no front.png / meta.yaml required.
scripts/import_cards.sh -> scripts/archive/
  * One-shot importer; ran once on 2026-04-17 and is kept for history.
build(scanner): regenerable cards.json + compile-order sentinel
scanner/build.py now also writes scanner/compile-input/ (numbered PNG
copies that the user uploads to the MindAR browser compiler) and
records the upload manifest in scanner/targets.compile-order.txt.
Subsequent runs diff the manifest and warn when targets.mind needs to
be recompiled — eliminates the silent index-mismatch failure mode
(audio plays for the wrong card after a recompile in different order).
cards.json is slimmer: dropped the redundant `youtube_id` field
(scanner reads only `audio` / `audio_url` now) and the duplicated
`compiled_reversed` / `target_images` debug fields.
Other changes:
  * build.sh writes scanner/build-info.json (sha+date) for the CF Pages
    deploy. The committed scanner/version.json (which nothing read) is
    deleted; the APK version lives in app/android/app/build.gradle.
  * PUBLIC_BASE_URL constant — was duplicated 17× per-card-row.
  * audio_url is mandatory (build.py raises if mp3 missing) so the
    scanner can drop its multi-step fallback chain.
refactor(scanner): split into ES modules, drop dead YouTube IFrame code
The single 1035-line inline IIFE in index.html became 305 lines + five
focused ES modules under scanner/lib/. Removed the YouTube IFrame Player
plumbing entirely (~150 lines): every card has had a local mp3 for some
time, the YT path was unreachable but still executing fragile lifecycle
code (player creation, ytReady tracking, pendingAudio queue) on every
boot, and would NPE if YT.PlayerState was ever read after the iframe
script tag was removed.
New layout:
  lib/storage.js       — try/catch wrapper around localStorage
  lib/homography.js    — quad→quad math + matrix3d helper
  lib/audio.js         — playback + Capacitor Filesystem cache
  lib/update-banner.js — APK version compare + ApkUpdater bridge
  lib/main.js          — DOM wiring + AR lifecycle
Side fixes:
  * Drop dead 'card.art ||' fallback (build.py always populates it).
  * Use optional chaining for window.Capacitor probes consistently.
  * Surface anim start() errors via the diagnostic version label
    instead of silently swallowing them.

fix(scanner): hide stray YouTube play overlay; force camera fullscreen [release] [publish]
Two visual bugs reported on v0.1.8 with screenshot:
1. The YouTube IFrame host was 200×200 at top:-220px, supposed to be
   off-screen, but Android Chrome was bringing the giant play
   overlay back into view. Reduce to a 1×1 px host with opacity

   visible size while still passing autoplay heuristics.

perf: ship 512² fronts + 256² cover, drop canvas DPR cap [release] [publish]
Two changes that compound:
- scanner/build.py now downsizes data/ → scanner/art/ via Pillow
  Lanczos. Originals at 1024² stay in data/ for print PDFs (printed
  card quality unaffected). scanner/art/ shrinks 46MB → 9.6MB.
  Card fronts: 512², ~530KB each. Cover: 256², ~107KB.
- scanner/animations/plane.js: DPR cap was already 1, kept that way.
  Each textured-tile draw now samples a 256² source instead of 1024²,
  a 16× cheaper texture sample, and the canvas backing buffer is
  4× smaller than at native DPR.
Net: plane animation should run smoothly during the 5s glide on
mid-range phones. APK shrinks correspondingly.

feat(app): main screen photo card honours brightness target
The home-screen device card was rendering raw bitmaps while
History + Viewer applied the normalisation filter — inconsistent.
DevicesViewModel now exposes brightnessTarget(deviceId), DeviceList
plumbs the flow into each row, the row decodes-and-measures p95
alongside the existing bitmap fetch, and ZoomableDevicePhoto
accepts a ColorFilter applied to the Image.
Same algorithm + clamps as the history viewer (Normalise.kt),
including the no-filter short-circuit when the scale would be
within 1% of identity.
Bumps app 0.9.11.
perf(app): cache history JPEGs on disk via Ktor HttpCache
History JPEGs at /devices/:id/history/<ts>.jpg are immutable by
design (the key IS the timestamp; the bytes never change). The
Worker already declares this with `Cache-Control: immutable,
max-age=86400`. Install Ktor's HttpCache with a FileStorage in
the app's cache dir so the second-and-subsequent loads of a
given timestamp return without any network round-trip at all —
not even a revalidation. latest.jpg is `no-store` so it bypasses
the cache and stays live.
Investigated whether to also cache p95 measurements in memory
keyed by (deviceId, tsMs). Decode dominates (~50–100 ms on UXGA)
vs measurement (~5 ms); not worth the LRU.
Bumps app 0.9.10.

feat(app): display-time brightness normalisation
Two-layer brightness consistency now:
1. Device-side calibration (unchanged): pins AEC/AGC at the sensor.
2. App-side normalisation (new): rescales each displayed JPEG so
   its 95th-percentile luma matches the user-picked target.
When the user swipes-right on a history frame to calibrate, the
app additionally measures that frame's p95 and persists it in
DataStore (per-device, local-only). All subsequent frame renders
in both the history strip and fullscreen viewer apply a
ColorFilter that scales toward the saved target, clamped to
0.6×–2.0× to keep broken frames from being amplified to noise.
Settings → Camera exposure card now distinguishes device-side
calibration from app-side brightness target. Two buttons:
- "Reset device" — clears server-side calibration (existing).
- "Reset in app" — clears local brightness target.
Shown only when each side has something to reset.
A new "Brightness target only" state covers the case where the
user removed device calibration but kept the local target — the
phone still normalises display brightness even with auto AEC.
Bumps app 0.9.9.

fix: rip out LDR low-light gate; fix reset-cue infinite loop [fw-release]
Two bugs reported by serial-monitor traces from a real device:
1. EVERY frame was being dropped as "low light" because the LDR
   reads 280–310 with the fridge bulb fully on, while
   CONFIG_FRIDGEYE_LDR_THRESHOLD_RAW was 1500. The threshold was
   set for the ULP-wake use case where it works fine; treating it
   as a "scene is dark enough to skip JPEG upload" signal was
   misguided. No frames reached R2 → history empty → calibrate-
   from-frame can't find metadata → calibration was effectively
   broken end-to-end.
   Camera AEC already handles dim scenes; the LDR added noise, not
   signal. is_low_light() and the dark-frame gate are gone.
   MIN_JPEG_BYTES still catches genuinely broken frames (sensor
   stuck → 2–5 KB output).
2. The reset-calibration cue was firing on every upload forever.
   applyCalibrationFromTelemetry's state-4→1 cleanup only cleared
   appliedCalibration when the device's reported AEC differed from
   what was previously applied. But auto AEC in stable lighting
   converges to the same numbers, so calibrationsMatch returned
   true and the cleanup never ran. The reset cue is single-shot
   from the device's perspective (calibration_clear + auto AEC);
   the next telemetry arriving at all proves it was processed.
   Cleared unconditionally now.
Bumps firmware 0.7.7.
Plain serial monitor doesn't need the build dir — that only matters
for panic-trace symbol decoding, which is rare enough not to
warrant two dedicated targets. The per-variant split inherited from
firmware-flash-* added confusion ("which one is my device?")
without benefit. For symbolicate-on-panic, call
`idf.py -B build-usb monitor` directly.

feat(app): smoother history swipe — haptic, alpha+scale ramp, camera+settings icon
Three changes to the history swipe-to-action UX:
1. Haptic tap at the commit threshold. Material AOSP haptics
   guidance: small single event paired with the visual cue, so the
   user knows "the action will fire if I let go now" without having
   to look at the screen.
2. Visual ramp during drag. Background colour alpha tracks
   dismissState.progress; icon scale ramps 0.6→1.0 with the same
   progress. Replaces the previous "invisible threshold" feel.
3. Lower positionalThreshold (50% default → 30%). Matches the
   Material example (25%) and common mail/messaging app behaviour.
Plus the swipe-right "calibrate from this frame" gets a proper
icon pair: a PhotoCamera vector drawable + Settings gear. Drawable
sits in res/drawable/ rather than pulling material-icons-extended
(~25 MB APK overhead avoided).
Bumps app 0.9.7.
fix(fw): fast-wifi failure falls back to full discovery in same call [fw-release]
The 0.7.5 fast-cache code only invalidated the cache on failure and
returned ESP_FAIL — meaning the *next* wake would retry with full
discovery, but THIS wake would give up. On a battery device that's
"door opened, fast path failed, no frame uploaded, sleep". One bad
cache entry = one missed door-open per device. Bad.
Refactor wifi_connect into a small try_associate(use_cache=bool)
helper called twice: fast path first, slow path on failure. Between
the two:
- esp_wifi_stop() so the second esp_wifi_set_config + start cycle
  doesn't race on stale state.
- DHCP restarted + static IP cleared so the slow path doesn't carry
  the bad pin into discovery.
- Cache invalidated so the next wake won't re-attempt the dead pin.
End result: a stale cache costs maybe an extra second of fast-path
attempt before the slow path runs, never a missed wake.
Bumps firmware 0.7.6.
perf(fw): cache BSSID + channel + static IP for faster cold boots [fw-release]
After a successful Wi-Fi associate, persist {ssid, bssid, channel,
ip, netmask, gateway, dns} into NVS. On the next wifi_connect with
the same SSID, pin BSSID + channel and assign the IP statically
before esp_wifi_start() — skipping channel scan and DHCP entirely.
Typical saving per cold boot: 2–4 s. For the battery build that's
material per wake; for the USB build it's faster fridge-open →
fresh-frame.
Safety: any cached-path failure invalidates the cache and restarts
DHCP for the current call, so the next wifi_connect falls back to
full discovery. Covers AP channel changes, DHCP lease expiry, router
swaps. SSID match guards against cache reuse after re-pair.
Bumps firmware 0.7.5.

refactor: four-state calibration model, drop LDR floor + baseline [fw-release]
Worker:
- DeviceRecord gains appliedCalibration (what the device confirmed
  it's using) alongside calibration (what the user wants). The pair
  drives the four-state UI: auto / set-awaiting / set / reset-
  awaiting. calibratePending field gone.
- New cue header X-FridgEye-Cmd-Reset-Calibration: 1, sent when the
  user removed the calibration server-side but the device hasn't
  acked yet. Device drops NVS and switches to auto on receipt.
- applyCalibrationFromTelemetry updates appliedCalibration purely
  from device-reported AEC/AGC (no more calibratePending bookkeeping).
- Calibration struct shrinks to {aecValue, agcGain, capturedAt} —
  ldrBaseline dropped (LDR too noisy to be a useful per-device
  signal). Wire format becomes "aec,agc".
- handleCalibrateDevice (the manual Calibrate-button endpoint) is
  gone. Only DELETE /calibrate (remove) and POST /calibrate/from-frame
  remain. Calibration is set exclusively from history swipe-right.
- LDR floor removed from handleCalibrateFromFrame: the user is
  looking at the photo and chose it; they don't need a gatekeeper.
Firmware:
- calibration_t loses ldr_baseline. NVS key bumps "cam_pin_v1" →
  "calib_v2"; old blobs are orphaned, device falls back to auto until
  user recalibrates from history.
- New X-FridgEye-Cmd-Reset-Calibration cue: clears NVS + enables
  auto AEC.
- is_low_light no longer takes a calibration_t* — global Kconfig
  threshold for everyone.
App:
- DeviceSummary gains appliedCalibration, loses calibratePending.
  CalibrationCard derives the four-state UI from
  (calibration, appliedCalibration). State 1 has no button (calibrate
  is set from history only). States 2/3 show values + Remove. State 4
  is informational (no button — server already cleared; just waiting).
- WorkerApi.startCalibration removed.
Bumps firmware 0.7.4, app 0.9.5.

refactor: rename pin → calibration, fix calibrate-from-frame propagation [fw-release]
Rename: the word "pin" was unclear (overlap with PIN codes etc).
End-to-end rename to "calibration" — the same word users already see
in the UI:
- Worker: CameraPin → Calibration. cameraPin field → calibration.
  encodeCameraPin → encodeCalibration. calibrationToPin →
  telemetryToCalibration. Cue header X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin →
  X-FridgEye-Cam-Calibration.
- Firmware: cam_pin.c → calibration.c. cam_pin_t → calibration_t.
  cam_pin_load/save/clear/parse → calibration_load/save/clear/parse.
  s_active_pin → s_active_calibration. s_have_pin →
  s_have_calibration. camera_apply_pin → camera_apply_calibration.
  upload_result_t.cam_pin_encoded → calibration_encoded.
- App: data class CameraPin → Calibration. cameraPin → calibration.
NVS key value stays "cam_pin_v1" for backward compatibility — already-
calibrated devices keep their stored values across the upgrade.
Propagation fix: decorateCues was sending both the calibrate cue AND
the cameraPin header during calibrate-from-frame. The firmware
processed the calibrate cue first (switching the sensor to AUTO,

fix: rip out LDR low-light gate; fix reset-cue infinite loop [fw-release]
Two bugs reported by serial-monitor traces from a real device:
1. EVERY frame was being dropped as "low light" because the LDR
   reads 280–310 with the fridge bulb fully on, while
   CONFIG_FRIDGEYE_LDR_THRESHOLD_RAW was 1500. The threshold was
   set for the ULP-wake use case where it works fine; treating it
   as a "scene is dark enough to skip JPEG upload" signal was
   misguided. No frames reached R2 → history empty → calibrate-
   from-frame can't find metadata → calibration was effectively
   broken end-to-end.
   Camera AEC already handles dim scenes; the LDR added noise, not
   signal. is_low_light() and the dark-frame gate are gone.
   MIN_JPEG_BYTES still catches genuinely broken frames (sensor
   stuck → 2–5 KB output).
2. The reset-calibration cue was firing on every upload forever.
   applyCalibrationFromTelemetry's state-4→1 cleanup only cleared
   appliedCalibration when the device's reported AEC differed from
   what was previously applied. But auto AEC in stable lighting
   converges to the same numbers, so calibrationsMatch returned
   true and the cleanup never ran. The reset cue is single-shot
   from the device's perspective (calibration_clear + auto AEC);
   the next telemetry arriving at all proves it was processed.
   Cleared unconditionally now.
Bumps firmware 0.7.7.
Plain serial monitor doesn't need the build dir — that only matters
for panic-trace symbol decoding, which is rare enough not to
warrant two dedicated targets. The per-variant split inherited from
firmware-flash-* added confusion ("which one is my device?")
without benefit. For symbolicate-on-panic, call
`idf.py -B build-usb monitor` directly.
feat(app): smoother history swipe — haptic, alpha+scale ramp, camera+settings icon
Three changes to the history swipe-to-action UX:
1. Haptic tap at the commit threshold. Material AOSP haptics
   guidance: small single event paired with the visual cue, so the
   user knows "the action will fire if I let go now" without having
   to look at the screen.
2. Visual ramp during drag. Background colour alpha tracks
   dismissState.progress; icon scale ramps 0.6→1.0 with the same
   progress. Replaces the previous "invisible threshold" feel.
3. Lower positionalThreshold (50% default → 30%). Matches the
   Material example (25%) and common mail/messaging app behaviour.
Plus the swipe-right "calibrate from this frame" gets a proper
icon pair: a PhotoCamera vector drawable + Settings gear. Drawable
sits in res/drawable/ rather than pulling material-icons-extended
(~25 MB APK overhead avoided).
Bumps app 0.9.7.
fix(fw): fast-wifi failure falls back to full discovery in same call [fw-release]
The 0.7.5 fast-cache code only invalidated the cache on failure and
returned ESP_FAIL — meaning the *next* wake would retry with full
discovery, but THIS wake would give up. On a battery device that's
"door opened, fast path failed, no frame uploaded, sleep". One bad
cache entry = one missed door-open per device. Bad.
Refactor wifi_connect into a small try_associate(use_cache=bool)
helper called twice: fast path first, slow path on failure. Between
the two:
- esp_wifi_stop() so the second esp_wifi_set_config + start cycle
  doesn't race on stale state.
- DHCP restarted + static IP cleared so the slow path doesn't carry
  the bad pin into discovery.
- Cache invalidated so the next wake won't re-attempt the dead pin.
End result: a stale cache costs maybe an extra second of fast-path
attempt before the slow path runs, never a missed wake.
Bumps firmware 0.7.6.
perf(fw): cache BSSID + channel + static IP for faster cold boots [fw-release]
After a successful Wi-Fi associate, persist {ssid, bssid, channel,
ip, netmask, gateway, dns} into NVS. On the next wifi_connect with
the same SSID, pin BSSID + channel and assign the IP statically
before esp_wifi_start() — skipping channel scan and DHCP entirely.
Typical saving per cold boot: 2–4 s. For the battery build that's
material per wake; for the USB build it's faster fridge-open →
fresh-frame.
Safety: any cached-path failure invalidates the cache and restarts
DHCP for the current call, so the next wifi_connect falls back to
full discovery. Covers AP channel changes, DHCP lease expiry, router
swaps. SSID match guards against cache reuse after re-pair.
Bumps firmware 0.7.5.

refactor: four-state calibration model, drop LDR floor + baseline [fw-release]
Worker:
- DeviceRecord gains appliedCalibration (what the device confirmed
  it's using) alongside calibration (what the user wants). The pair
  drives the four-state UI: auto / set-awaiting / set / reset-
  awaiting. calibratePending field gone.
- New cue header X-FridgEye-Cmd-Reset-Calibration: 1, sent when the
  user removed the calibration server-side but the device hasn't
  acked yet. Device drops NVS and switches to auto on receipt.
- applyCalibrationFromTelemetry updates appliedCalibration purely
  from device-reported AEC/AGC (no more calibratePending bookkeeping).
- Calibration struct shrinks to {aecValue, agcGain, capturedAt} —
  ldrBaseline dropped (LDR too noisy to be a useful per-device
  signal). Wire format becomes "aec,agc".
- handleCalibrateDevice (the manual Calibrate-button endpoint) is
  gone. Only DELETE /calibrate (remove) and POST /calibrate/from-frame
  remain. Calibration is set exclusively from history swipe-right.
- LDR floor removed from handleCalibrateFromFrame: the user is
  looking at the photo and chose it; they don't need a gatekeeper.
Firmware:
- calibration_t loses ldr_baseline. NVS key bumps "cam_pin_v1" →
  "calib_v2"; old blobs are orphaned, device falls back to auto until
  user recalibrates from history.
- New X-FridgEye-Cmd-Reset-Calibration cue: clears NVS + enables
  auto AEC.
- is_low_light no longer takes a calibration_t* — global Kconfig
  threshold for everyone.
App:
- DeviceSummary gains appliedCalibration, loses calibratePending.
  CalibrationCard derives the four-state UI from
  (calibration, appliedCalibration). State 1 has no button (calibrate
  is set from history only). States 2/3 show values + Remove. State 4
  is informational (no button — server already cleared; just waiting).
- WorkerApi.startCalibration removed.
Bumps firmware 0.7.4, app 0.9.5.

feat: camera exposure pin + dark-frame skip + fullscreen history viewer [fw-release]
Device side:
- New cam_pin module (NVS-backed) holds AEC value + AGC gain + LDR
  baseline. Applied on every boot after camera_init, before the first
  capture, so a calibrated device always uses the same exposure.
- camera_capture reads back s->status.aec_value / agc_gain + LDR raw
  per frame; values land in the multipart telemetry JSON.
- is_low_light(ldr_raw, pin) decides per frame: pre-calibration uses
  the global FRIDGEYE_LDR_THRESHOLD_RAW (kconfig now common to both
  builds); post-calibration uses pin.ldr_baseline / 2. Dark frames
  skip the JPEG upload entirely and ping /status instead, so the
  device stays "online" without overwriting the latest good frame.
- New cue headers: X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate (one-shot) and
  X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin "aec,agc,ldrBaseline" (always-on while a pin
  exists). Firmware persists the pin and re-applies on the next
  capture; no reboot needed.
- ldr_read_raw moved out of battery.c into a USB-buildable ldr.c TU
  so both builds can sample without dragging in the rest of battery.h.
- Firmware bumped to 0.7.0 (lockstep with the Worker constant — the
  version-sync CI gate enforces this).
Worker side:
- CameraPin stored on the KV DeviceRecord; POST /calibrate stages
  the cue, DELETE /calibrate clears both pending state and any
  existing pin. Calibration result is captured when a /upload or
  /status response carries aecValue+agcGain+ldrRaw AND the LDR raw
  is bright enough (MIN_CALIBRATION_LDR_RAW=1200) — refuses to pin
  from a dim frame so the user gets a clean retry on the next try.
- Telemetry validators added for aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw,
  lowLight, skippedDarkFrames. All optional, all range-checked.
App side:
- DeviceTelemetry + DeviceSummary gain the new fields. Camera
  exposure card on settings: Calibrate / Recalibrate / Unlock with
  the pinned values shown as InfoRows. UI flips immediately on
  request (refresh-after-dispatch) so the user sees "pending" without
  waiting for the next watch tick.
- History strip honours the saved zoom/rotation so frames render the
  same way as the main card.
- Tapping a history frame opens a new fullscreen viewer: no
  rotation applied, fit-to-width so the long axis hits both screen
  edges, pinch-zoom (1x..8x) + drag-pan, translucent topbar with
  the local capture time. Zoom state is discarded on back — by
  design, history is read-only.
- App bumped to 0.8.0.
feat(worker): camera-pin + calibrate cue + dark-frame telemetry
Adds the server side of "lock the camera exposure once, skip dark
frames":
- New CameraPin (AEC value + AGC gain + LDR baseline + capturedAt)
  stored on the KV DeviceRecord. Always shipped back on upload/status
  responses as X-FridgEye-Cam-Pin so the firmware persists+applies
  it. Encoded as "aec,agc,ldrBaseline" for a cheap on-device parse.
- POST /devices/:id/calibrate (Google-authed) sets a sticky cue. The
  device picks it up via X-FridgEye-Cmd-Calibrate, runs AEC normally
  on the next capture, and reports converged values in the same
  upload's telemetry JSON (aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw). Worker turns
  that into a pin, clears the cue. Refuses calibrations from a dim
  frame (ldrRaw < 1200) so a fridge-bulb-off capture doesn't pin
  to garbage.
- DELETE /devices/:id/calibrate clears both pending state and any
  existing pin — the UI "Unlock" action.
- Telemetry gains aecValue, agcGain, ldrRaw, lowLight,
  skippedDarkFrames. All optional, all validated; AWB stays auto
  since the OV2640 status struct doesn't expose per-channel gains.
Firmware + app land in follow-ups.
feat: per-device history of last 10 frames
Worker keeps a ring buffer at users/<sub>/<id>/history/<ms>.jpg, written
alongside the existing latest.jpg on every multipart upload. Two new
endpoints serve the app: GET /devices/:id/history (timestamps,
newest-first) and GET /devices/:id/history/<ts>.jpg (one frame).
History is wiped together with the rest of the device record on
unlink confirm.
App gets a History card on the device settings screen → a vertical
list of frames with timestamps. Motivates the "is the dark frame just
the door closing?" diagnostic — the trend across the last 10 frames
makes that obvious.
Bumps to 0.7.0.

fix(voice): await speak completion so cheer isn't cut off
FlutterTts.speak() resolves the moment playback starts, not when the
utterance finishes. The previous await was a no-op: we'd start the
cheer, immediately call stop() and speak() with the score, killing
the cheer mid-word.
awaitSpeakCompletion(true) flips that so speak() resolves only when
the platform-side queue marks the utterance complete. Now: cheer
plays in full → score is announced after it.

feat(names): no two players share a first letter

unique within a single roster. If the constraint can't be met
(unlikely with 26 letters and 103 names, but possible for huge
rosters), it falls back to plain uniqueness, then finally to
duplicates.
Same rule applies on the home screen when re-rolling a single
player or adding a player: avoids same-letter clashes with the
other current players. With 8 players (the max), we cover at most

rule almost always succeeds.

release: 0.13.2 — regenerate icons via shared recipe
Voicer's launcher icon is now produced by the shared
flutter_common/scripts/icon/generate_icon.py recipe. The hand-painted
PNGs are replaced with recipe output; visually indistinguishable on the
adaptive layers (mean per-channel error 2.9), close on the legacy
composite. Going forward any future re-theme runs the recipe on a
modified colour set rather than hand-editing PNGs.
Inputs that drive voicer's icon now live in two files at the repo
root:
- icon-glyph.png       the white mic shape (extracted from the
                       original icon.png so the legacy composite
                       matches the hand-painted version's stroke
                       width)
- icon.config.yaml     6 colour stops + geometry ratios
Run the recipe:
  python3 ../flutter_common/scripts/icon/generate_icon.py \
      icon.config.yaml
  dart run flutter_launcher_icons
See ../flutter_common/scripts/icon/README.md for the full schema.

release: 0.13.0 — voice cloning via Plachta/Seed-VC
Adds neural voice cloning to playlists. Record 15-30 s of a friend, the
voice becomes a Voice choice in the picker. Select it on a playlist
track and the first playback POSTs the recording + reference to the
public Plachta/Seed-VC HuggingFace Space, caches the converted WAV on
disk, and plays it. Subsequent plays of the same (recording, voice)
pair are instant.
Architecture:
- Voice gains an optional cloneReferencePath field pointing at the 30 s
  reference audio in app documents/voices/. Forward-compatible JSON.
- VoiceClone (lib/voice_clone.dart): three-step Gradio client —
  multipart upload, predict POST returning event_id, SSE stream until
  the complete event carries the WAV URL. No auth (the Space is public).
- CloneCache (lib/clone_cache.dart): sha1 of (recording.path, voice.id)
  → File under Documents/clone-cache. Pure on-disk; persists across
  launches.
- CloneOrchestrator (lib/clone_orchestrator.dart): single in-process
  coordinator — coalesces concurrent requests for the same pair, calls
  cache then network, surfaces user-facing status.
- VoiceCloneCaptureScreen: dedicated 30 s mic capture, lives under a
  new "Clone a voice" entry next to "Voice tone match" (LTAS, kept
  available but renamed to match what it actually does).
- PlaylistDetailScreen resolves clone-capable voices via the
  orchestrator before each playback; bakes the cloned audio into the
  player path, so pitch/formant/LTAS are zeroed out for that pair (the
  cloning already produced the final timbre).
Trade-offs honestly documented:
- Network dependency: Plachta's Space is public and free. Rate limit
  + cold-start are the failure modes; first call after sleep may take
  ~60s. Failures fall back to playing the original track so playlists
  don't stall.
- Privacy: voice samples + recordings leave the device for inference.
  Acceptable for the personal/family use case this was built for.
- Quality varies with the reference sample's cleanliness. The capture
  screen tells the user to record 15-30 s of clean voice without
  background noise.
LTAS-based "Match a voice" is kept but renamed "Voice tone match" to
honestly describe what it does (spectral tilt copy, not identity clone).
Adds http (1.2.x) and crypto (3.0.x) dependencies.

release: 0.12.0 — offline STT via whisper.cpp + recording fix
Replaces the live SpeechRecognizer-during-recording with offline
whisper.cpp transcription after the file is saved. The simultaneous
SpeechRecognizer was stealing the mic stream on Android 13+ Pixels
(ALT.AudioRecordSource silently detaches MediaRecorder), so recordings
since 0.9.0 produced empty files even though the UI showed timer
ticking.
The new flow: recording saves with a timestamp name immediately, then
a background isolate decodes the AAC, resamples to 16 kHz mono, and
hands it to whisper.cpp (multilingual base.q5_1, ~57 MB asset). On
success the row renames in place to the transcribed phrase.
Language picker is back at the top of Recordings with persisted
choice. 'Auto' lets whisper detect from the encoder output; explicit
codes (cs, en, sk, de, pl, …) skip detection and force the language.
Three-dot menu on each recording gets an 'Auto-name' entry to re-run
whisper on existing clips (e.g. after changing the language).
Other changes:
- AndroidManifest now uses screenOrientation=fullUser; rotation
  follows the system auto-rotate toggle and the OS shows the rotate
  suggestion icon when the device is turned with auto-rotate off.
- Voice list rows show 'matched tone' instead of 'pitch 0 · formant 0'
  for LTAS-only matched voices.
- Update banner moved from red (read as error) to amber on near-black.
APK size 58.8 MB → 120 MB (whisper.cpp + ggml-cpu + base model).

release: 0.11.0 — voice matching via LTAS-based EQ
Adds a "Match a voice" entry to the Sounds picker. Pick an existing
recording, give it a name, and a 64-bin log-spaced Long-Term Average
Spectrum (LTAS) is computed from it and stored on the Voice. At playback
the source clip's LTAS is derived inline from WORLD's CheapTrick output
(no extra analysis pass) and the corrective filter
H(f) = clamp(target/source, +-12 dB) is multiplied into each spectrogram
frame before resynthesis.
This is a tone-colour match — formant landscape and spectral tilt — not
voice cloning: rhythm, intonation, and glottal character are unchanged.

release: 0.10.0 — keep screen on while live monitoring
Drop the screen-off problem in live mode. While the user is in Live
Start state, voicer adds FLAG_KEEP_SCREEN_ON to the activity window
so the lock never fires. AudioEngine.stop() clears the flag.
The same flag is cleared on activity onDestroy as a safety net, so a
crash mid-live doesn't leave the screen pinned forever (the activity
is dying anyway, but the window manager honours the flag right up to
destroy).
Playback paths (recordings list player, playlist play-all) also call
stop(), which clears the flag. Clearing a flag that was never set is
a no-op — safe.
Not done: a full foreground service. That would survive task removal
and OS-initiated kills. The current approach handles "phone in pocket
auto-locks" but does not handle the user swiping voicer off the
recents stack. A foreground service is a bigger commit (notification
channel, MediaSession glue) deferred to a focused PR.

release: 0.9.2 — one-line auto names; drop transcription field
The recogniser's output is the name. There's no other use for the
text, so drop the (newly added, never read) `transcription` field on
Recording — the name carries the same information.
Cap the auto-name to ~60 characters on the row. Flatten any newlines
the recogniser emits, back up to the previous space when the cut
would land mid-word, ellipsise when truncated. The existing
`maxLines: 1` on the row title is the second line of defence in case
something sneaks past.
A long recording still gets a short title; the audio on disk is
untouched.
release: 0.9.1 — cap speech recognition at 40 s
If you record for three minutes the row title shouldn't be three
minutes of words. Hard-stop the recogniser at 40 s of capture; the
mic recording itself keeps going to whatever length the user wants.
Behaviour:
- start() schedules a Handler callback at CAPTURE_LIMIT_MS.
- The callback flips `running` false and calls `stopListening()` on
  the recogniser. The accumulated text is what stop() will return.
- stop() and destroy() remove the pending callback so it doesn't
  fire later as a no-op.
Drop the post-hoc label-truncation helper. With the capture itself
bounded the transcription returned to Dart is already row-friendly,
and the call site stores the same value as both the name and the
full transcription.
release: 0.9.0 — speech-to-text labels + language picker
Recording auto-labelling:
- SpeechRecognizerService wraps Android's on-device SpeechRecognizer,
  driven from MainActivity alongside the AudioRecorder so the
  transcription is ready by the time the user taps Stop. It re-arms
  after each result so longer recordings are captured as joined
  phrases.
- When recording ends, the resulting Recording carries a `transcription`
  field (nullable; persisted in recordings.json) and the auto-name
  defaults to that phrase, with the existing timestamp pattern as the
  fallback when recognition was unavailable or silent.
- Recording row: top line shows the (transcribed) name, subtitle shows
  a compact "d.M.y H:mm · Ns" stamp.
Language picker:
- New SpeechLanguageStore persists the user's BCP-47 selection in
  SharedPreferences. The platform discovers available languages via
  ACTION_GET_LANGUAGE_DETAILS; if the speech service strips that, we
  fall back to the device's configured locales.
- Recordings screen has a "Language: …" row at the top with a Change
  button. Picking "Device default" clears the override.
AndroidManifest gains a `<queries>` entry for `android.speech.
RecognitionService` so SpeechRecognizer.isRecognitionAvailable returns
true on Android 11+.
No transcription on a clip just means timestamp-only in the row; no
user-facing error is surfaced.
fix(playlist): hide effect UI when a Voice is overriding it
When a non-identity Voice is selected on a track, the WORLD pre-render
path takes over and the engine runs in Clean mode — the stored
effectId never reaches the audio chain. Three places still showed it:
- Track edit dialog: the Effect card stayed visible (greyed out) with
  "Disabled while a Voice is selected". Now it's omitted entirely when
  a voice is active; reset the voice to None and the picker reappears.
- Track row label: tags read "Woman • Robot 75%" even though Robot was
  inert. Now reads "Woman" only.
- Track row inline slider: dragged intensity changed a value nobody
  read. Slider is suppressed when a voice is active.
The data model still stores the chosen effect — pick a voice, change
your mind, the effect returns. This is intentional: lets the user
toggle a voice on and off without losing their effect choice.
release: 0.8.0 — simplify UI: kill Settings, unify Sounds picker
Bottom nav drops from 5 tabs to 3: Live, Recordings, Playlists.
Settings tab is gone. Its only useful action ("Check for updates") moves
to a `⋮` overflow on the Live app bar. Version stays accessible via the
in-app update banner when relevant. The "About latency" paragraph was
already a redundant copy of the route banner that the Live screen shows
in context — deleted.
Voices tab is gone. Voices fold into a single unified Sounds picker on
the Live screen with three labelled sections: Voices (Woman, Man,
Child, Old Man, Giant, Tiny, Monster, Helium), Effects (the remaining
20 character/space effects), Custom (user-saved voices, with a "+
Build custom voice" entry routing to the existing builder screen).
Catalog trim:
- 8 pitch-only effects (Pitch Up/Down, Chipmunk, Deep, Monster, Helium,
  Child, Gender Up/Down) are hidden from the default picker because
  they duplicate Voice presets that produce a more recognisable result
  via WORLD.
- 2 Robot variants (Big Robot 40 Hz, Small Robot 220 Hz) are hidden
  because Robot at 60 Hz covers the slot.
- All 10 hidden IDs remain alive in the C++ engine and the catalog —
  old playlists that reference them still play exactly as before. The
  new `VoiceEffect.hidden` flag controls picker visibility only.
Picker change is purely UX. No persisted data shifts; `voiceId` and
`effectId` on a TrackItem still resolve through the same byId lookups.
release: 0.7.2 — close remaining audit findings
Workflow permissions:
- Workflow default is contents: read; the single job that builds and
  releases self-elevates to contents/actions: write. PRs no longer hold
  write tokens.
Dependency pinning:
- jirip_app pinned to the commit SHA the jirip_app-v0.1.0 tag pointed
  to, not to the moveable tag. Bumps now require an explicit SHA update.
R8 + resource shrinking on release builds:
- isMinifyEnabled and isShrinkResources are on. A proguard-rules.pro
  keeps Flutter's plugin registrant and NativeAudio JNI symbols, which
  R8 cannot prove reachable. Release APK 63.5 MB -> 58.8 MB.
Magic durations named:
- New lib/playback_timing.dart exports kDecoderWarmup, kEofPollInterval,
  kRecordingTick. Three screens now share these instead of inlining
  100/150/250 ms literals.
Listener-callback naming:
- _onChange / _onStore renamed to _refresh across all screens; one
  convention for "the store told us something changed."
MediaCodec null guards:
- The three `!!` non-null assertions in FilePlayback now use
  requireNotNull with messages (matches the team rule). The mime-type
  case is restructured to keep the non-null value in scope instead.
Hygiene:
- .gitignore covers Thumbs.db / desktop.ini.
- README lists all Makefile targets (build-aab, emulator, clean were
  missing).
release: 0.7.1 — clean-code audit fixes
Stores no longer wipe user data on a corrupt JSON file:
- PlaylistStore/VoicesStore/RecordingsStore.load now validate the root
  is a List, skip individual bad entries with a debugPrint, and leave
  the file untouched on a decode failure (next save still overwrites,
  but the user can rescue the file off-device first).
C++ engine hardening:
- PitchShifter explicitly deletes copy/move (heap-allocated SoundTouch
  Impl; the implicit copy ctor would have double-deleted).
- FormantPitchTransformer's two double** matrices now live in a
  DoubleMatrix RAII wrapper (std::vector-backed) so any throw between
  CheapTrick and the end of transform() no longer leaks rows.
- Named kF0Floor constant replaces the two identical `71.0` literals.
CI:
- All third-party action references pinned to commit SHAs (with the
  major tag preserved as a trailing comment).
- key.properties is written by printf instead of an unquoted heredoc.
  Heredoc was both interpolating `$` from secret values and carrying
  YAML indent whitespace into the produced properties file.
- gradle.properties -Xmx4G replaces -Xmx8G; the previous value
  exceeded the GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runner's 7 GB.
Build / signing:
- release build now logs a loud warning when key.properties is missing
  and it falls back to the debug key (CI flow must always provide the
  keystore — silent fallback was a foot-gun).
Engine API cleanup:
- AudioEngine.cancelPlayback collapsed into stop() — they were
  identical native handlers; one Dart method is clearer. Native side
  drops the duplicate `cancelPlayback` case.

release: v0.1.0
First public build of SlotWatch.
Features:
- Saved queries (date + time window + sport + venues + duration).
- Each query toggles between watching (notify on newly-free slots) and
  viewer-only (run on demand against any date).
- Bundled venue: Infinit Step (memberzone.cz) with tennis, squash,
  badminton, table tennis.
- Self-describing venue capabilities — adding a venue requires no
  changes outside its own folder.
- In-app self-update via jirip_app.Updater.
v0.1.0 ships without background scheduling; pull-to-refresh on the
viewer screen runs the scraper. Background mode + notifications come
in v0.1.1.
feat(venues): self-describing capabilities + multi-sport Infinit Step
Each Venue now exposes a VenueCapabilities object declaring which sports
it offers, with per-sport resources/durations/working hours. The query
editor reads the union of all venues' capabilities to build its sport
list and intersects offerings to pick valid durations; no UI code
hardcodes any venue facts.
InfinitStepVenue now lists tennis, squash, badminton, and table tennis.
Each sport has its own DevExpress menu index and resource ID range
(badminton keeps the verified 22000085-92 first, plus unverified
22000058-69 that the site's CSS knows about — labels are generic until
we can validate them against the live UI).
The earlier 'courtCount' getter is gone; UI reads
offering.resources.length instead. SlotQuery does NOT gain
resourceIds — per the design decision, callers don't filter by court.
fix(android): add R8 keep rules for Play Core deferred-component classes
R8 fails minifyReleaseWithR8 because Flutter's embedding references
com.google.android.play.core.* classes for deferred-component support,
and we don't depend on Play Core. We don't use deferred components, so
keep them out of the classpath and silence R8 with -dontwarn + -keep.
If deferred components are ever added, replace these rules with the
real 'com.google.android.play:core' dependency.
